campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council reviewed the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods like buses, bikes, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Multimodal transportation improvements (buses, biking, pedestrian access)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule they're considering. After hearing from residents, they're moving forward with the proposal and plan to vote on officially adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being introduced
Council expedited the process by skipping initial procedural steps
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from several March meetings and authorize the Town Manager to hire QLM, Inc. for a construction project. The meeting focused on administrative tasks like confirming previous meeting minutes and moving forward with a construction contract.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March 2026 meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and plan upcoming council meetings scheduled for April 21 and April 28, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to the agendas, decided which items needed specific time slots, and handled any legal notice requirements. The council also reviewed official records from early April.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21 council meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 28 draft agenda
Set specific time slots for agenda items if needed
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a study session to review Mountain View's plans and budget for building and improving city projects over the next year. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's construction and improvement projects budget
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City planning and capital spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting notes from March and discuss changes to tenant protections rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing laws to strengthen protections for residential renters.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to amend tenant protections program rules
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code
Residential renter protections and housing regulations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were given time to speak about topics they cared about (limited to 3 minutes each). The council also held elections to choose a new Chair and Vice Chair, and began discussing the city's budget plan for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any topic
Chair and Vice Chair elections for city leadership
Fiscal year budget development and planning
State law limits council decisions on topics not officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important information about legal deadlines for residents who want to challenge city decisions. If the city makes a decision that affects you, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes official to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, according to state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
State law governs legal challenges to city actions
Quasi-adjudicative decisions covered by this rule
Meeting disclosure requirements for residents

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative items, including updating the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and reviewing new projects that individual council members want to add to the city's work schedule. They also discussed changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan update (2025-2030 Consolidated Plan)
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to how city council fills vacant seats
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The property owner wants to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space as an incentive for preserving and earthquake-proofing the historic structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
Downtown Commercial zone development incentives
